untranslatable_reason: This signal requires options-specific indicators (implied volatility, calendar spread width, option Greeks) which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The signal describes derivative pricing behavior that cannot be expressed using equity price action, moving averages, or technical oscillators alone. Implied volatility and calendar spread P&L mechanics require options-chain data.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_353 is a theoretical options trading rule from McMillan's volatility analysis. It explains that calendar spreads widen when IV increases because long-dated options have higher vega exposure. This requires real-time IV surface data and options pricing models, not available in price-based technical analysis. Consider integrating VIX derivatives or IV indexes (like india_vix) as a proxy, but this would only approximate the underlying mechanism.
